title: scutellaria glutinosa
intro: Scutellaria glutinosa, also known as the Sticky Skullcap, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, belonging to the mint family and characterized by its square stems and small blue or purple flowers. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Scutellaria glutinosa has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating insomnia, and soothing digestive problems, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects. Scutellaria glutinosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
